title: S2 pixel-oracle checkpoints not camera-registered: full-frame native-vs-ares diff dominated by warp/force-player viewpoint mismatch (frame-spanning parallax cluster)
repro: Cache stock PPM (ares) for dam_glass_visual_probe, then: sense_pixel_sweep.sh --route dam_glass_visual_probe --binary <rel> --ares-bin <ares>. Verdict on real Dam frame: 777 clusters, 775 auto-attributed (three_point_filter/vi_filter), 2 UNEXPLAINED. Worst = bbox[0,19,640,442] area=97576 mean_delta=34.1 = frame-spanning. PROVEN a viewpoint artifact NOT a renderer defect: (1) native-vs-native identical params = 0 clusters (deterministic); (2) same native renderer/position with ONLY the look-target moved to the stock coords (GE007_AUTO_FACE_COORD_SCRIPT 14051.53:-474.70:1545.11) reproduces the identical frame-spanning signature bbox[0,19,640,442] area=183952 mean_delta=33.1. The route's own actor guard also fails (chr 11 pos delta 13.95>12.0). Criterion-2 per-stage closure needs camera-REGISTERED checkpoints (identical camera matrix both sides); do NOT allowlist and do NOT file as parity-divergence.

history.note: Filed by the FID-0044 pixel-oracle-sweep increment. This is the true blocker to a clean per-stage criterion-2 verdict: the only existing pixel checkpoint is not viewpoint-registered tightly enough for a full-frame native-vs-ares diff. Do NOT resolve by loosening APPROXIMATIONS.md bounds (would mask real defects).
